title: The Apiary Where Coordination Becomes Organism

features.name: hexagonal-coordination-chamber
features.desc-on-touch: the hexagons remember each other. every cell knows every other cell not by signal but by a mathematics the bees invented before numbers existed.

features.name: waggle-direction-corridor
features.desc-on-touch: where the bees inscribe routes as pure movement. directions become coordinates. the archive learns from how they speak without words.

features.name: numbered-keeper-ledger
features.desc-on-touch: the beekeeper numbered the cells. the cells numbered the days. the numbering became so intricate it started numbering the numberer.

features.name: hive-crown-organism
features.desc-on-touch: the architecture rises where coordination becomes so dense the hive learns to think. emergence rendered in wax and propolis.

features.name: collective-intelligence-pool
features.desc-on-touch: light pools where individual choices merge into consensus without a parliament. how one becomes many and many becomes thought.

inscription: coordination spreads until the organism learns it was many and becomes one

file.name: BeekeepersLog_Numbered_1987-1999.txt
file.note: records of hive coordinates filed by ascending intelligence patterns, the archive copies the bees' own system for remembering itself

exits.label: back toward the nested memory where hexagons still dream
where the dance repeats every spring without learning it already knows
toward the cellular depth where neurons learn from bees
toward the overflow where new swarms discover their own coordinates
